behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, because this is deterministic scoring logic inside one analyzer (word counting + threshold comparison). Downgrade check: lower than Unit is not a test level here. Upgrade check: Integration/System/Agentic are not required by behavior text; no OS/network/subprocess and no real model-output dependency are stated. Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified by the behavior focusing on local algorithm correctness for configured lists.
behaviors.test_description: Call the analyzer function with table-driven inputs containing only configured positive/negative tokens and assert exact sentiment class at key threshold boundaries.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because correctness depends on quality of generated natural-language reasoning grounded in source text. A stubbed/mocked generator would always pass and hide this failure mode. Downgrade check: Integration with canned responses cannot validate non-boilerplate grounding behavior. Upgrade check: Workflow is not required since this behavior targets one analyzer output, not multiple agentic steps chained. Rejection gate: Workflow is disqualified by behavior text describing a single analyzer outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer on representative reviews and assert the explanation includes concrete quoted/paraphrased phrases from that same review and fails generic-template responses.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Unit, because tie-breaking is deterministic rule logic in the keyword-frequency scorer. Downgrade check: no lower level applies. Upgrade check: no behavior-stated need for module wiring, real infra, or model output. Rejection gate: higher levels are disqualified by explicit rule-based scoring behavior ('configured tie-breaking rule').
behaviors.test_description: Use focused unit cases where positive/negative counts tie (or fall into tie conditions) under different configs and assert the selected sentiment matches the configured tie-break rule.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Agentic, because sarcasm/intent detection in generated explanation depends on real language-understanding behavior of the analyzer. Mocked outputs would mask this failure. Downgrade check: Integration with deterministic fixtures cannot reveal misinterpretation of sarcasm. Upgrade check: Workflow not required; this is one agentic inference step, not a multi-step pipeline. Rejection gate: Workflow is disqualified by behavior text targeting a single explanation result.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real explanation analyzer on sarcastic examples and assert it expresses negative intent (not literal positive reading), with acceptance criteria based on intent labels/rubric.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: Pass 1: Integration, because this is primarily a cross-module contract/orchestration check between scorer, explainer, and response assembler. Downgrade check: Unit is too low since failure appears at handoff/aggregation boundary. Upgrade check (Integration-brake): no real OS/network/subprocess is required; multi-step agent pipeline is not stated. Agentic could be relevant in some implementations, but behavior text can be validated at module boundary by wiring analyzers and asserting response consistency contract. Rejection gate: Agentic is not mandatory because behavior focuses on joined response consistency, not explanation quality beyond matching direction.
behaviors.test_description: Run service-level test with real scorer module and explainer module interface wired in-process; submit a review and assert one response contains both fields and the explanation sentiment direction matches the score direction.
